title: varlink: ListContainerMounts does not include container ids
body:
`podman mount`, with no arguments, displays the CID associated with each mount point. The varlink interface does not seem to provide that:
```
# podman run --name aaa alpine touch /aaa
# podman run --name bbb alpine touch /bbb

# podman mount aaa
/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/d1bf0bf2caffb0f988ab00013e3099fbaead6e5c97b3924ba499eaa54e486540/merged
# podman mount bbb
/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/ba09b6b232c726b116142ac41a2909366a6d97273572957479a45e531ecf6f27/merged

# varlink call unix://run/podman/io.podman/io.podman.ListContainerMounts
{
  "mounts": [
    "/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/d1bf0bf2caffb0f988ab00013e3099fbaead6e5c97b3924ba499eaa54e486540/merged",
    "/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/ba09b6b232c726b116142ac41a2909366a6d97273572957479a45e531ecf6f27/merged"
  ]
}
# podman mount
36dbeea4e49e /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/d1bf0bf2caffb0f988ab00013e3099fbaead6e5c97b3924ba499eaa54e486540/merged
b17b00bb284b /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/ba09b6b232c726b116142ac41a2909366a6d97273572957479a45e531ecf6f27/merged
```
podman 7838a13b61e built from master

comments.body: @edsantiago the PR did returned a map of ids and mounts. is this sufficient?
@baude I had hoped for a list as described in my comment on that PR, but this will probably work. Thanks again for your work on it.
